rationale: Clear, well-organized, and plainly written. Each paragraph advances a distinct argument (efficiency, reliability, congestion counterargument, economics, equity) with smooth transitions. Language is direct without being flat. Loses a point on specificity: it name-drops New York and Bogotá but offers no actual data—no travel-time percentages, ridership figures, or cost savings—so the case relies on general assertions rather than concrete evidence. The piece is also visibly truncated mid-sentence at the end, which undercuts the conclusion and slightly hurts brevity/economy (the space used doesn't fully land). Voice is competent and engaged but leans toward polished-template op-ed rather than distinctly human or memorable.
